fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer physical therapist license to connecticut, physical therapist compact connecticut, connecticut physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Connecticut DPH — PT Licensure
board.mailing_address: CT Department of Public Health, Practitioner Licensing and Investigations Section, 410 Capitol Ave., MS #12HOS, P.O. Box 340308, Hartford, CT 06134-0308

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Connecticut.

steps.title: Create an eLicense Account and Apply
steps.description: Register for an account on the CT DPH eLicense portal and complete the online application for initial PT licensure, paying the required $200 application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your physical therapy educational institution to send official transcripts directly to the Connecticut DPH. Electronic transcripts are preferred if available.

steps.title: Pass National and Jurisprudence Exams
steps.description: Ensure official scores for the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) are reported to Connecticut, typically through FSBPT. Additionally, pass the Connecticut Jurisprudence Exam, also administered through FSBPT.

steps.title: Provide Licensure Verification (if applicable)
steps.description: If you are or have been licensed as a Physical Therapist in another state, arrange for a verification of licensure to be sent directly from each state's licensing authority to the CT DPH.

steps.title: Await DPH Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Department will review your complete application and all supporting documentation. Once approved and verified, your Connecticut Physical Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Connecticut
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Connecticut, submit your application and supporting documents through the Connecticut Department of Public Health's **eLicense** online system.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the CT DPH eLicense portal, click 'Register for a New Account', and follow the prompts to create your user ID and password before initiating a new license application.
